yesterdayAnalysis: Saturday's outdoor ride logged only 17.9mi with no TSS or HR data captured—significantly short of the planned 45-50mi long ride that was the week's focal workout. This represents a major gap in the weekly plan and likely explains why weekly TSS is at 392/420 (93%). Missing this key aerobic endurance session is concerning as we're in week 5 of 10 in the build phase.
todayRecommendation: Complete the planned 10-11mi long run today to maintain run volume progression (you hit 11mi on May 22, so fitness is there). Given the missed long bike yesterday, consider adding the scheduled open water swim to salvage weekly volume, but prioritize run quality and don't force extra work if recovery is compromised.
weekProgress: Week is 28 TSS short of the 420 target primarily due to Saturday's abbreviated ride. Discipline balance shows only 2 bike sessions totaling 77 TSS this week (should be 3-4 sessions with ~150-200 TSS), while run volume is solid with 11mi + 5mi + brick runs completed. One brick workout completed (May 24) meets minimum weekly requirement.
keyInsight: The missed long outdoor ride is a critical gap in your aerobic base development—these 3-4 hour endurance sessions at race intensity are non-negotiable for Ironman preparation and can't be easily replicated by shorter indoor sessions.
